Description: Default/public ACR Task pool is Azure-managed compute and is not inside the customer VNet, which may raise security boundary concerns if used for sensitive or internal builds.
Response: Limit default/public pool usage to bootstrap or non-sensitive build activities. Use the dedicated private ACR agent pool for internalized builds requiring private network access.

Description: Security gate thresholds may not be strict enough, allowing vulnerable images to progress to nonprod or production.
Response: Define Trivy severity thresholds, fail conditions, exception handling, and POA&M requirements before promotion.

Description: Trivy and JFrog Xray may identify different findings, creating potential blind spots if only one scanner is treated as authoritative.
Response: Treat Trivy as an additive CI/CD gate, not a replacement for existing Xray controls. Compare scanner results during rollout.

Description: GitHub Actions, ACR Tasks, AKS, or ACR availability issues could delay image builds or promotions.
Response: Document retry procedures, manual break-glass process, and dependency ownership.

Description: An image could be promoted before all nonprod runner groups are validated.
Response: Require runs-on validation across all required runner groups before promotion gate approval.

Description: Generated POA&M entries may lack enough context for security review or audit use.
Response: Define required POA&M fields, ownership, severity mapping, evidence links, and review workflow.

Description: Current process builds runner images separately by environment, creating inconsistency between nonprod and prod and duplicating work.
Response: Implement a build once, validate in nonprod, promote to prod image promotion model.

Description: Approved routine maintenance still requires someone to manually log in during an off-hours window and click a workflow.
Response: Replace manual execution with scheduled workflow execution, gated promotion, and automated validation.

Description: Basic runner image maintenance requires repeated security review board approval, slowing normal patching and maintenance cycles.
Response: Create a pre-approved automated maintenance pattern with evidence-based gates and defined exception handling.

Description: JFrog Xray may block future pulls, but a build can continue after an image was already pulled, creating a CI/CD enforcement gap.
Response: Add a Trivy-based CI/CD security gate that evaluates the built artifact before nonprod deployment or production promotion.

Description: Current manual process does not consistently capture who approved the image, what was built, what was scanned, and what was deployed.
Response: Capture commit SHA, image digest, scan result, SBOM, POA&M output, approvals, deployment status, and release metadata.

Description: Security team must review and approve scan thresholds, fail criteria, exception handling, and POA&M output.
Response: Schedule security review and document the approved security gate policy.

Description: Security and platform teams must review the use of the default/public ACR Task pool for bootstrap and the dedicated private ACR agent pool for internalized builds.
Owner: Security Team / Platform Team / My Team
Response: Present the boundary diagram and document allowed use cases for each ACR agent pool.

Description: GitHub OIDC federation and Azure RBAC permissions must be configured for ACR, AKS, and deployment actions.
Response: Create federated credentials and assign least-privilege Azure RBAC roles.

Description: Dedicated private ACR agent pool must be attached to the correct VNet/subnet and allowed to reach internal JFrog, internal CA, ACR, DNS, and required services.
Owner: Network Team / Platform Team / My Team
Response: Validate routing, DNS, firewall rules, private endpoints, and required egress paths.

Description: Repository dispatch workflow must exist and accept image version tags for downstream Terraform/Helm deployment.
Response: Implement dispatch payload contract and downstream workflow.

Description: Nonprod and production runner groups must have validation workflows that confirm new images can pick up jobs successfully.
Response: Create runs-on validation workflows for all required runner groups.

Description: Build logs, SBOMs, scan reports, POA&M artifacts, and release metadata need an approved storage and retention location.
Response: Define approved evidence repository, retention period, and access control.

Description: Approve moving from per-environment image builds to a build once, validate, and promote model.
Response: Approve the nonprod-to-prod image promotion model as the target operating model.

Description: Determine what vulnerability severity levels block promotion and what findings require POA&M or exception handling.
Response: Approve Trivy/security gate policy, severity thresholds, and exception process.

Description: Decide whether the default/public ACR Task pool is acceptable for bootstrap builds only.
Owner: Security Team / Platform Team / Voting Members
Response: Approve limited use of the default/public pool for non-sensitive bootstrap stage only.

Description: Approve use of the dedicated private ACR agent pool for internalized builds that require internal CA, JFrog mirror, and private configuration.
Owner: Security Team / Platform Team / Voting Members
Response: Approve private ACR agent pool as required for sensitive/internal image build stages.

Description: Determine what event marks an image as production approved: promotion gate approval, production validation success, or GitHub release creation.
Response: Define the official release approval point and update process documentation.

Description: Determine whether generated POA&M artifacts are stored as pipeline evidence initially or integrated directly into a formal GRC process.
Response: Approve initial POA&M handling approach and future integration path.

Description: Initial POA&M entries may be generated as files or pipeline artifacts rather than integrated directly into a GRC platform.
Response: Accept for initial release to deliver immediate visibility, with a future enhancement to integrate into formal GRC tooling.

Description: First implementation may use Trivy as the primary CI/CD gate while JFrog Xray remains separate.
Response: Accept for initial release to provide immediate CI/CD enforcement while planning future scanner correlation.

Description: Promotion may initially require manual approval in GitHub rather than full integration with enterprise change management tooling.
Response: Accept for controlled rollout while formal change-management integration is defined.

Description: Exceptions may initially be documented in POA&M artifacts or review comments rather than a fully automated risk acceptance workflow.
Response: Accept for initial release so the security gate can launch without waiting for full exception workflow automation.

Description: Bootstrap build may use Azure-managed default/public ACR Task compute outside the customer VNet.
Response: Use only for low-sensitivity bootstrap build to reduce complexity. Internalized builds use the private ACR agent pool.
